nanette-barragan.notes_set: Federal evidence finalization 2026-06-05. Textbook progressive CA-Dem party-line voting pattern, NO cross-aisle catches found in the rubric-aligned direction. CHC Chair tenure (118th Congress) gives her institutional leadership on immigration-restraint opposition — HARD FALSE on border axis. Operates as pro-choice despite Catholic upbringing (Planned Parenthood Action Fund endorsed; NARAL 100% career rating; SBA Pro-Life scorecard tracks her opposing Hyde/Born-Alive). Voted YES on RFMA per her own house.gov 'Marriage Equality' press release. Voted YES on Israel supplemental H.R.8034 roll 152 (4/20/24) — NOT one of the ~40 D NAY-crossers; own house.gov release framed it as 'stand with our democratic allies' — interventionist hawk = HARD FALSE on FP restraint. AIPAC $49,522 in 2023-24 cycle per OpenSecrets = HARD FALSE on FP q3 (no foreign-lobby donations). BSCA YES with full D caucus + own press release applauding signing = HARD FALSE on self-defense. SAVE Act NAY — as CHC Chair was vocal opponent calling it red-bait (own house.gov press release). No specific PAS/euthanasia, embryonic-stem-cell, ABA/sound-money/CBDC, pharma-mandate, NCVIA-repeal, Big-Ag, raw-milk, Pentagon-audit positions located — null on those high-bar cells.

derek-tran.notes_set: Federal evidence finalization 2026-06-05. First-term Vietnamese-American Army-veteran Democrat. TWO HARD CROSS-AISLE catches: (1) Laken Riley Act H.R.29 YEA on h6 (1/7/25) — one of ~46 D crossovers on detention of undocumented immigrants accused of theft; (2) June 2025 resolution thanking law enforcement (including ICE) YEA — drew criticism from Vietnamese immigrant-rights group VietRISE. Additionally split party March 2025 to vote YEA on a Chinese Communist Party higher-ed foreign-influence bill (national-security hawk; doesn't map to RESOLUTE FP cells). Standard progressive on Pillar A — Reproductive Freedom + Equality caucus memberships HARD; Born-Alive 119th vote (h27) NAY HARD; SAVE Act h102 NAY HARD; GVPTF membership HARD-FALSE on self-defense. Buddhist self-ID per Wikipedia — christian_liberty q0 null (the cell asks about affirming the right to publicly profess Christ, not personal Christian identity; no position located). Heritage Action 119th 0% noted in background. THIN 119th-only record so most pre-119th marquee cells (Born-Alive pre-2025 / RFMA / Equality Act / FRA / Israel-supplemental / BSCA / HR2) pre-date him — null with confidence_federal because caucus-membership + first-Congress voting pattern is documented.

lou-correa.notes_set: Federal evidence finalization 2026-06-05. CRITICAL CALIBRATION FINDING: The pre-research hypothesis that Correa would be a textbook Blue-Dog cross-aisle correction did NOT hold. His Blue-Dog co-chair role, Catholic faith, and Border Security Ranking Member seat suggested deviations — but on the marquee R bills he voted with the D caucus every time. Quote: 'I'm a Catholic. I have my personal views on that, but I am also a member of Congress who represents my constituents' (Wikipedia). NRA-PVF F, NARAL 100%, SBA Pro-Life F, Heritage Action 0%/5%. The genuine 'deviation' is mildly hawkish tone on border-technology legislation he himself introduced (passed committee with GOP support) — but his ROLL-CALL pattern is standard D. Cosponsor of both RFMA H.R.8404 (HARD YEA both passages) AND Equality Act H.R.5 117th (HARD YEA per own press release). H.R.5 Parents Bill of Rights NAY + amendment to bar immigration-status disclosure at schools. H.R.2 NAY (own press release as Ranking Member opposing). Laken Riley NAY (own FB statement: 'bad public policy'). BSCA YES with full D caucus. SAVE Act NAY (own press release + 2026 successor opposition). Trump 2nd impeachment YEA. Jan 6 he hid in the gallery with a gas mask + supported impeachment + 25th Amendment invocation. Correa is the Blue-Dog-brand-without-the-Blue-Dog-vote-record case. NO marquee cross-aisle catches found.

profile.background: Korean-American · Episcopalian per Pew Research 119th Congress affiliation tracker · UCI School of Law professor · former Schumer Senate Banking Committee counsel + Center for American Progress director for financial markets policy + SEC enforcement attorney · former CA state senator SD-37 · first-term US House (sworn Jan 2025) · CPC member · CPC Anti-Corruption Task Force chair · author of CA SB 264 (gun-show ban on state property)

dave-min.notes_set: Federal evidence finalization 2026-06-05. First-term progressive Democrat; UC Irvine law professor + former CA state senator who authored multiple gun-control bills as state senator. TWO notable catches: (1) HARD CROSS-AISLE on Laken Riley Act h6 (1/7/25) YEA — one of 46 D crossovers; (2) RARE FP-RESTRAINT TRUE — campaign site explicitly states he has been 'standing up against Trump's illegal and reckless war in Iran' (anti-Iran-strike June 2025) — TRUE on FP q0 (Article I war-powers requirement). Pillar A standard progressive: campaign-site issue page features 'Reproductive Rights' + Senate 'secure women's access to reproductive healthcare' accomplishment + Planned Parenthood of OC/SB Action Fund 2026 endorsement + Reproductive Freedom For All (NARAL successor) 2024 endorsement + Equality California 2024 endorsement. Self-described CA Senate accomplishment 'ban the sale of guns on all state property' = CA SB 264 author = HARD FALSE on self-defense (both q0 and q1). SAVE Act h102 NAY HARD. Sanctuary stance: campaign-site states he has been 'a loud and consistent voice in calling out the cruel and often criminal actions of ICE agents' = HARD FALSE on BI q2. CPC Anti-Corruption Task Force chair + sponsors congressional stock-trade-ban legislation — doesn't cleanly map to RESOLUTE IC q4 (Pentagon-audit/defense-contractor framing). Episcopalian per Pew tracker but no public Christian-witness statements in office located — CL q0 null. Heritage Action 119th expected ~0% (page returned 500 in sweep; included in sources but not cell-scored). NB on currency: confirmed Prop-50 did NOT redistrict him out; advanced primary.

set.office: Former 2026 No Party Preference candidate · U.S. Representative California 48th congressional district · withdrew candidacy 2025-08-23 via YouTube announcement · did NOT appear on the June 2 2026 top-two primary ballot (Jim Desmond R / Marni von Wilpert D advanced)

profile.background: Retired investment banker / corporate treasury officer · born East Chicago IN · Purdue BA → Univ. of Chicago Booth MBA · first political run · self-styled 'balanced, centrist' independent · No Party Preference per California Secretary of State; FEC committee 'ALBERT J MORA FOR THE PEOPLE' (C00899278) listed Unaffiliated · withdrew candidacy 2025-08-23 via YouTube announcement · campaign site albertjmora.com offline as of 2026-06-05

albert-james-mora.notes_set: Federal STATUS FIX 2026-06-05. Mora withdrew his CA-48 candidacy on August 23, 2025 via YouTube announcement (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=kQw60aQUBdY) — approximately ten months before the June 2, 2026 top-two primary. He did NOT appear on the primary ballot per Ballotpedia: 'will not appear on the ballot for the general election on November 3, 2026.' Jim Desmond (R) and Marni von Wilpert (D) advanced from the June 2 primary; Issa endorsed Desmond on March 6 2026. status=former + candidacy_status=not_running. Status fix is the high-value win — removes a stale challenger from the active queue. No policy positions located before withdrawal: campaign website (albertjmora.com) is currently offline (consistent with a withdrawn-candidate domain lapse); his Ballotpedia Candidate Connection survey contained governance-process platitudes only ('understand the job description,' 'two-way communication,' 'serves with gratitude and dignity') and the constituent-concerns narration paragraph ('reproductive rights, secure border, humane immigration policy, sensible gun control, support of military, environmentalism') is third-person reporting of what voters told him, NOT his stated stances — null per evidence-first cardinal rule. All 50 federal cells null on no-evidence basis.
